3. Look at `contributing/add-new-provider.md` for guidelines
4. Check `packages/provider/src` for the LanguageModelV3 or ImageModelV1 interfaces
5. Look at how `createPixelArt`-style factory functions are structured (e.g., `createOpenAI` in packages/openai)
6. Check the root tsconfig.json to understand how packages are referenced

1	# Add new provider
2	
3	## `@ai-sdk/<provider>` vs 3rd party package
4	
5	Every provider is welcome to create a 3rd party package. We are happy to link to it from our documentation.
6	
7	If you would prefer a 1st party `@ai-sdk/<provider>` package, please create an issue first to discuss.
8	
9	## Example
10	
11	https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8136/files
12	
13	## How
14	
15	1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
16	2. Set version in `packages/<provider>/package.json` to `0.0.0`
17	3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
18	4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts` depending on what model types the provider supports
19	5. Add documentation in `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/<last number + 10>-<provider>.mdx`
20	
21	See also [providers.md](providers.md)
22	
23	## When in pre-release mode
24	
25	If `main` is set up to publish `beta` releases, no further action is necessary. Just make sure not to backport it to the `vX.Y` stable branch since it will result in an npm version conflict once we exit pre-release mode on `main`
26

## New `@ai-sdk/pixelart` provider

### Files created (14 new files, 1 modified)

#### Package config (`packages/pixelart/`)
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata, version `0.0.0`, scripts, exports |
| `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript project config, references `provider` & `provider-utils` |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Disables `composite` for tsup builds |
| `tsup.config.ts` | Bundles CJS + ESM with type declarations, injects `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` |
| `turbo.json` | Turborepo task config |
| `vitest.node.config.js` | Node.js test runner config |
| `vitest.edge.config.js` | Edge runtime test runner config |

#### Source (`packages/pixelart/src/`)
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `version.ts` | Exposes `VERSION` constant injected at build time |
| `pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` union type (`'pixelart/standard'`, `'pixelart/retro'`, `'pixelart/8bit'`) and `PixelArtStyle` |
| `pixelart-image-options.ts` | Zod schema for provider options: `style` (`'pixel' \| 'retro' \| '8bit'`) and `pixelDensity` (1–32) |
| `pixelart-error.ts` | `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` using `createJsonErrorResponseHandler` |
| `pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel` implementing `ImageModelV3` — POSTs to `/images/{modelId}/generate`, handles `size` → `width`/`height`, `style`, `pixel_density`, downloads image binaries |
| `pixelart-provider.ts` | `createPixelArt()` factory + `pixelart` default instance; only `image()`/`imageModel()` supported — `languageModel` and `embeddingModel` throw `NoSuchModelError` |
| `index.ts` | Public barrel exports |

#### Modified
- **`tsconfig.json`** (root) — added `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` in alphabetical order between `perplexity` and `provider`

### Usage example
```ts
import { createPixelArt } from '@ai-sdk/pixelart';
import { generateImage } from 'ai';

const pixelart = createPixelArt({ apiKey: process.env.PIXELART_API_KEY });

const { image } = await generateImage({
  model: pixelart.image('pixelart/standard'),
  prompt: 'A pixel art castle at sunset',
  size: '512x512',
  providerOptions: {
    pixelart: { style: 'retro', pixelDensity: 8 },
  },
});
```
